name: when-optimizing-prompts-use-prompt-architect
description: Comprehensive framework for analyzing, creating, and refining prompts for AI systems using evidence-based techniques
capabilities: Detect and eliminate anti-patterns (vague instructions, missing context)
Apply evidence-based techniques (CoT, Self-Consistency, ReAct, Few-Shot)
Run A/B tests to measure improvement
Iteratively refine prompts based on validation results
Generate optimization reports with performance metrics
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: Pure documentation-only skill containing no executable code, network calls, or filesystem access. All 160 static findings are false positives - the scanner misinterpreted shell script examples in markdown documentation as command execution threats, JSON metadata URLs as network calls, and legitimate terminology as C2/cryptographic patterns. This is a methodology framework for prompt engineering.
valueStatement: Poorly structured prompts produce inconsistent AI responses. This skill applies a 5-phase framework using Chain-of-Thought, Self-Consistency, and other evidence-based patterns to transform prompts into high-performing versions with measurable improvement.
userTitle: Optimize AI prompts with evidence-based techniques
limitations: Requires claude-flow@alpha dependency for full integration
20-40 minutes needed for complete optimization cycle
Effectiveness may vary across different AI models
User must provide the initial prompt to optimize

useCases.title: Optimize agent system prompts
useCases.description: Improve system prompts for specialized agents to produce more consistent outputs

useCases.title: Enhance writing prompts
useCases.description: Create prompts that reliably generate high-quality content with predictable formatting

useCases.title: Structure data extraction prompts
useCases.description: Build prompts that consistently extract structured data from unstructured sources

promptTemplates.title: Quick Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this prompt for structure, clarity, and anti-patterns. List the 7 components present or missing: [INSERT PROMPT]

promptTemplates.title: Structure Template
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an optimized prompt using this template: System Context, Role Definition, Task Description, Constraints, Format Specification, Examples, Quality Criteria. Topic: [INSERT TOPIC]

promptTemplates.title: CoT Enhancement
promptTemplates.prompt: Add Chain-of-Thought reasoning to this prompt. Include step-by-step thinking structure with thinking tags: [INSERT PROMPT]

promptTemplates.title: Full Optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply the complete 5-phase Prompt Architect framework to optimize this prompt. Include anti-pattern detection, structure optimization, evidence-based techniques, A/B test design, and iterative refinement. Original prompt: [INSERT PROMPT]

bestPractices: Always use the 7-component structure: System Context, Role, Task, Constraints, Format, Examples, Quality Criteria
Replace vague language with imperative commands: Use 'You must' instead of 'Please try to'
Include 2-3 concrete examples demonstrating expected input-output patterns
Define clear quality criteria with measurable success thresholds
antiPatterns: Using vague qualifiers: 'please', 'try to', 'maybe', 'possibly' - these weaken instructions
Missing system context: Failing to provide background information or domain knowledge
No output format specification: Allowing unstructured or inconsistent response formats
Conflicting instructions: Including requirements that contradict each other

faq.question: Which AI tools support these optimized prompts?
faq.answer: Optimized prompts work with Claude, GPT-4, Codex, and any LLM. Structure patterns are model-agnostic.

faq.question: What improvement should I expect?
faq.answer: Typical improvements range from 20-40% in score, 85-95% success rate, and 0.8-0.9 consistency.

faq.question: How does this integrate with SPARC workflows?
faq.answer: Use prompt-architect before SPARC phases to optimize specification prompts for better architecture outcomes.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill processes prompts locally in your workflow. No prompt data is sent to external services.

faq.question: What if my optimized prompt fails validation?
faq.answer: Phase 5 handles failures through iterative refinement. Analyze root causes, apply fixes, and re-validate.

faq.question: How is this different from basic prompt engineering?
faq.answer: This uses a systematic 5-phase framework with A/B testing. You get measurable improvements, not just best practice tips.
